plugin_desc: APRESIA managed switch-specific SNMP collection for the APRESIA NP series (AEOS-NP platform, e.g. ApresiaNP3000/NP4000/NP7000, IANA PEN 278). In addition to standard IF-MIB interface metrics, it collects APRESIA-NP-HW private OIDs for device health — control-plane CPU utilization (npCpuUtilizationValue, the master device CPU usage as a 0-100 percentage), chassis temperature in degrees Celsius (npTemperatureCurrent, read directly with no scaling), per-fan status (npFanStatus) and per-power-supply status (npPowerStatus). The CPU utilization table carries three fixed rows for the same master CPU averaged over the past 5 seconds, 1 minute and 5 minutes; since telegraf inputs.snmp cannot filter rows, the metric is aggregated with max() per device to surface a representative reading. Fan and power-supply raw codes are normalized via telegraf processors.enum to 1=healthy / 2=fault (power-supply empty(3) means a slot is unpopulated and is treated as healthy), and the worst component is taken per device. The APRESIA-NP-HW private tree only exposes memory through npMemoryUtilizationFailureStatusCode, which is an OCTET STRING valid only when bound to a trap and not a pollable gauge, so memory utilization is not collected (N/A). Supported brands/models — APRESIA NP series (ApresiaNP2000/NP2100/NP2500/NP3000/NP4000/NP5000/NP7000) running the AEOS-NP firmware.
name: Switch

metrics.name: device_cpu_usage
metrics.description: This metric reports the CPU utilization of the APRESIA device control plane (APRESIA-NP-HW npCpuUtilizationValue, the master device CPU), expressed as a percentage from 0 to 100. The npCpuUtilizationTable carries three fixed rows for the same master CPU averaged over the past 5 seconds, 1 minute and 5 minutes; because telegraf inputs.snmp cannot select a single row, the metric is aggregated with max() per device to surface a representative reading. Sustained high CPU usage can indicate control-plane overload, routing instability, or attack traffic, and may lead to slow management response or packet processing delays.

metrics.name: device_temperature_celsius
metrics.description: This metric reports the chassis temperature in degrees Celsius of the APRESIA device (APRESIA-NP-HW npTemperatureCurrent). The object value is already in degrees Celsius and is read directly without scaling. The npTemperatureTable is indexed per unit and per sensor; the APRESIA NP4000 series carries one sensor per device, and the value is aggregated with max() per device so multi-sensor models surface the hottest reading. High temperatures can indicate cooling failure or airflow obstruction in the cabinet and risk thermal damage.

metrics.name: device_fan_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the fan health of the APRESIA device, derived from the per-fan status table (APRESIA-NP-HW npFanStatus, one row per fan). The raw codes (normal(1), abnormal(2)) are normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy and 2 = fault, and the worst fan is taken per device. A value greater than 1 indicates a failed fan that risks thermal damage and warrants investigation.

metrics.name: device_psu_state
metrics.description: This metric reports the power-supply health of the APRESIA device, derived from the per-power-supply status table (APRESIA-NP-HW npPowerStatus, one row per power supply). The raw codes (inOperation(1)=OK, failed(2)=fault, empty(3)=slot unpopulated) are normalized via telegraf processors.enum so that 1 = healthy and 2 = fault, with empty(3) treated as healthy because an unpopulated slot is not a fault, and the worst supply is taken per device. A value greater than 1 indicates a failed power supply that warrants investigation.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCInOctets
metrics.description: This metric indicates the average rate of bytes received over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCInOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifInOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: interface_ifHCOutOctets
metrics.description: This metric calculates the average rate of bytes sent over the past 5 minutes, measured in bytes per second, from the 64-bit IF-MIB ifHCOutOctets counter (ifXTable). The 64-bit counter avoids the rapid wrap of the 32-bit ifOutOctets on high-speed switch ports, keeping the rate accurate.

metrics.name: snmp_uptime
metrics.description: This metric indicates the uptime of the device since the last restart. By monitoring the uptime, network administrators can understand the stability and reliability of the device and identify potential reboot or failure issues.
